statement.markdown:
Given a sorted array of integers, implement a binary search algorithm to find the index of a target element. If the target element is not present, return -1.

Your function should handle cases where the target element is not present and should not return any incorrect index.

You can assume that the input array is sorted in ascending order.
statement.inputFormat: Input is an array of integers and a target integer.
statement.outputFormat: Return the index of the target element if it exists, -1 otherwise.
statement.notes: Time complexity: O(log n), where n is the size of the input array.

examples.explanation: Target element 2 is present in the array at index 1.
Target element 6 is not present in the array.

functionSignature.cpp: bool findIndex(std::vector<int>& nums, int n, int target);
functionSignature.java: public boolean findIndex(int[] nums, int target);
functionSignature.python: def findIndex(self, nums: list[int], n: int, target: int) -> bool:
